mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate signals an active easing cycle that lowers discount rates and stabilizes financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average and Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which should pressure equities lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger fires, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth proxy RSAFS is falling while housing starts HOUST also contract, aggregate demand is weakening across consumers and construction, so SPX should underperform over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either single-series proxy in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative when both series weaken together.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO indicates industrial production contraction and inventory liquidation, which should coincide with negative near-term equity returns as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if the 5-day forward return after the trigger is not negative on average or if the signal fails to beat a zero-return benchmark in at least two rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates both output and spending are weakening, which should keep equity returns negative over the next quarter as revenue expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal does not produce significantly lower 60-day returns than either input alone, or if sign accuracy falls below 55% in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate relative to recent history tightens financial conditions and raises the equity discount rate, so when policy remains above a restrictive threshold SPX should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the restrictive-rate regime does not coincide with negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate when FEDFUNDS is above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, labor income and household confidence weaken together, creating a recession signal that should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal does not improve predictive power over UNRATE alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not negative after simultaneous payroll losses and unemployment increases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST signals rebuilding of production and construction activity, supporting positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint recovery signal fails to produce positive average 20-day returns during recovery periods or if the effect disappears once either proxy is removed.
